headline: School Canteen Food Safety Violations Found at 28% of Schools
context: MOH and MOE joint audit found food safety violations in canteens at 2,856 of 10,200 schools inspected in 2025.

cards.big: 28% of school canteens failed food safety inspections conducted in 2025
cards.sub: Common violations include improper food storage expired ingredients and pest presence

cards.big: 1,245 food poisoning incidents reported at Malaysian schools between 2023 and 2025
cards.sub: Average incident affects 35 students with some outbreaks hospitalizing over 100

cards.big: School canteen operators bid for contracts based on lowest price not quality standards
cards.sub: Procurement model incentivizes cost-cutting at the expense of food safety

cards.big: Japan's school lunch program mandates certified nutritionists at every school since 1954
cards.sub: Systematic approach produces zero food poisoning outbreaks in school meal programs

cards.big: Feeding children unsafe food in schools is a failure of the most basic duty of care
Should school canteen contracts require food safety certification rather than lowest bid
